summary: Blazing Stranger>Franklin's Tower sequence & tight second‑set flow
blurb: Second set ignites with a blistering Feel Like A Stranger → Franklin's Tower jam, followed by an inspired Estimated Prophet into Eyes Of The World. Jerry’s guitar is on fire and Brent’s electric piano adds depth throughout.
review: The March 13, 1982 Reno show is a standout from the early‑'80s era, largely thanks to a second set that rides a wave of pure energy. The band launches into a scorching Feel Like A Stranger → Franklin's Tower sequence that reviewers describe as “blistering” and “letting loose,” with Jerry Garcia delivering manic, fast‑paced solos and gritty tones. From there the momentum never falters – Estimated Prophet rolls straight into an expansive Eyes Of The World, then bursts through Space into a soaring The Other One before closing the set with Black Peter and a rousing Around & Around. Brent Mydland’s electric piano provides salty texture that lifts the jams, while Phil Lesh’s four‑string bass “drops bombs” and fuels the drive. Even a false start on He’s Gone is quickly recovered, underscoring how locked‑in the group feels. The show’s highlights are captured best on the clean soundboard recording (gd1982-03-13.s2.sbd.digitalrbb.miller.112858.flac16), which offers crystal‑clear instrument separation and preserves every nuance of this high‑octane performance.

rating_calculation.sentiment_rationale: Strong positive sentiment across three recordings (positive) and one mixed recording, with repeated praise for the Stranger>Franklin's Tower jam, Jerry’s “letting loose” guitar work, Brent’s textured keyboards, and overall high‑energy second set flow. No consistent negative comments about performance quality were found.

best_recording.reason: Excellent soundboard capture with crystal‑clear instrument separation, preserving the full impact of the second‑set jams.

key_highlights: Blistering Feel Like A Stranger → Franklin's Tower jam that drives the entire second set.
Seamless transition from Estimated Prophet into Eyes Of The World and then Space → The Other One.
Brent’s electric piano adds salty, gruff texture that lifts the improvisations.

band_performance.Jerry: Delivers fast, manic solos on the Stranger → Franklin's sequence and crisp, gritty tones throughout; recovers quickly from a false start and keeps the band’s energy soaring.
band_performance.Phil: Provides powerful four‑string bass that “drops bombs,” driving the high‑energy second set.
band_performance.Bob: Locks in tight vocal and guitar exchanges with Jerry during the Stranger → Franklin's jam, adding strong harmonies.
band_performance.Brent: Electric piano shines with a salty, gruff texture, supplying depth and color to the jams, especially on Space and The Other One.
